title: C++ STL Vector
content:
### std::vector in Detail

`std::vector` is a dynamic array that provides fast random access and automatically manages its size.

---

### Memory Management

```cpp
vector<int> vec;
vec.reserve(100);  // Pre-allocate capacity for 100 elements

cout << vec.size();       // 0 (actual elements)
cout << vec.capacity();   // 100 (allocated space)
```

---

### Adding Elements

| Method | Description | Complexity |
|--------|-------------|------------|
| `push_back(val)` | Add to end | Amortized O(1) |
| `emplace_back(args...)` | Construct in-place | O(1) |
| `insert(pos, val)` | Insert at position | O(n) |
| `emplace(pos, args...)` | Construct at position | O(n) |

```cpp
vector<int> v = {1, 2, 3};
v.push_back(4);              // {1, 2, 3, 4}
v.emplace_back(5);           // {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
v.insert(v.begin() + 1, 99); // {1, 99, 2, 3, 4, 5}
```

---

### Removing Elements

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `pop_back()` | Remove last element |
| `erase(pos)` | Remove at position |
| `erase(first, last)` | Remove range |
| `clear()` | Remove all elements |

---

### Erase-Remove Idiom

```cpp
vector<int> v = {1, 2, 3, 2, 4, 2, 5};

// Remove all 2s
auto it = remove(v.begin(), v.end(), 2);
v.erase(it, v.end());

// Or in one line:
v.erase(remove(v.begin(), v.end(), 2), v.end());
```

---

> **Key Point:** Use `reserve()` to pre-allocate memory when you know the approximate size. This avoids repeated reallocations and improves performance.

quiz.question: What is the amortized time complexity of push_back()?
